action.hypothesis: The lower-LR gated sweep was a clean regression, so the next narrow exploitation move is to test the remaining untried capacity point on the best stable family. Reducing gated `EXPANSION_FACTOR` from 16 to 4 at K=128 may reveal a non-dominated quality-cost tradeoff and will close the last obvious one-parameter sweep on the current anchor before returning to broader architecture work.
action.summary: Run a gated K=128 proxy stabilization pass with `EXPANSION_FACTOR=4` on the current signum baseline, keeping LR, AUXK, and architecture fixed.
action.self_review: This keeps to one parameter, follows the current exploitation guidance, and resolves the remaining obvious gated sweep dimension rather than introducing another unstable architecture prototype. The main risk is a clear FVU regression from reduced capacity, but that result would still be informative because it closes the current gated sweep and sharpens the case for returning to architecture exploration.
notes_to_memory: `LR=2e-4` was a healthy but worse gated K=128 run, so lower-LR exploitation does not currently look promising.
This round closes the remaining obvious one-parameter gated sweep by testing the untried `EXPANSION_FACTOR=4` point on the strongest stable anchor.
Keeping architecture, K, optimizer, LR, and AUXK fixed isolates expansion factor as the only changed variable and gives a direct read on the quality-capacity tradeoff at the anchor K.
next_hypotheses: If `EXPANSION_FACTOR=4` is unexpectedly competitive, keep it alive as a quality-cost tradeoff candidate and consider checking runtime or memory benefits before discarding it.
If `EXPANSION_FACTOR=4` is clearly worse, conclude that the obvious gated one-parameter sweep is exhausted and resume architecture exploration or fix the blocked `factorized_topk` sanity issue.
If `EXPANSION_FACTOR=4` materially improves proxy FVU, promote it to full and treat lower-width gated training as the new mainline stabilization candidate.
